title: Claude Code Command
description: Frontmatter schema for Claude Code custom slash commands (`.claude/commands/*.md`). Commands are the **legacy format** for [skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) — they support identical frontmatter fields and behavior. New projects should prefer the skill format (`.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md`) for better organization and support for companion files. A file at `.claude/commands/review.md` and a skill at `.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md` both create `/review` and work the same way. If a skill and a command share the same name, the skill takes precedence. Use `$ARGUMENTS`, `$ARGUMENTS[N]`, or `$N` for argument substitution in the body.

name.description: Display name for the command using lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only (max 64 characters). This becomes the `/slash-command` name. If omitted, defaults to the file name without extension (e.g. `review.md` creates `/review`).

description.description: Describes what the command does and when to use it. Claude uses this to decide when to automatically load the command, so write it as a **triggering guide** — include concrete examples of user requests that should activate it. Command descriptions are always loaded into Claude's context so it knows they exist, but full command content only loads when invoked. If omitted, defaults to the first paragraph of the markdown body. Note: descriptions share a context budget (2% of the context window, minimum 16KB). Override with the `SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET` environment variable.
examples: Deploy the application to a target environment.
Fix a GitHub issue by number. Use when the user provides an issue number.
Review a pull request for quality and correctness.

argument-hint.description: Hint shown during `/` autocomplete to indicate expected arguments. Purely cosmetic — does not enforce argument structure. Arguments are accessed via `$ARGUMENTS`, `$ARGUMENTS[N]`, or `$N` in the markdown body.

model.description: Which [Claude model](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config) to use when this command is active. If omitted, inherits from the current session.

allowed-tools.description: Tools Claude can use **without asking permission** when this command is active. Built-in tools: `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Bash`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `Task`. Supports patterns like `Bash(git *)` to allow only specific subcommands. MCP tools use the pattern `mcp__<server>__<tool>`. If omitted, all tools are available with normal [permission prompts](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions). This field only grants auto-approval — it does **not** override denials.

oneOf.description: Comma-separated list of tool names. Supports patterns like `Bash(git *)`.

disable-model-invocation.description: When `true`, prevents Claude from automatically loading this command based on its description — the command's description is **not** included in Claude's context, so Claude will never know it exists unless the user invokes it. The command can only be triggered manually via its `/slash-command`. Use for manual-only workflows like `/deploy` or `/commit`.

user-invocable.description: When `false`, hides the command from the `/` autocomplete menu so users cannot invoke it directly. The command can still be loaded automatically by Claude based on its description. Use for background knowledge that Claude should apply silently.

context.description: Set to `fork` to run the command in an isolated [subagent](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents) context instead of inline. When forked, the command's markdown body becomes the subagent's **task prompt**. The subagent runs in a separate conversation fork with its own context window — it does **not** have access to the main conversation history.

agent.description: Which subagent type to use when `context` is set to `fork`. Built-in types: `Explore`, `Plan`, `general-purpose`. Can also be the name of a custom agent defined in `.claude/agents/`. Ignored if `context` is not `fork`. Defaults to `general-purpose`. See [built-in subagents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#built-in-subagents).

hooks.description: Lifecycle [hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) scoped to this command. Hooks defined here only fire when this command is active and are cleaned up when the command is unloaded. See [hooks in skills and agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#hooks-in-skills-and-agents).

Hooks.description: Map of [hook event](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#hook-events) names to arrays of matcher groups.

SessionStart.description: Fires when a session starts. Matcher filters by source: `startup`, `resume`, `clear`, `compact`. Command hooks have access to `$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` for [persisting environment variables](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#persist-environment-variables).

UserPromptSubmit.description: Fires when the user submits a prompt, before Claude processes it. **No matcher support** — always fires.

PreToolUse.description: Fires before a tool is executed. Matcher filters by tool name (e.g. `Bash`, `Edit|Write`, `mcp__.*`). See [PreToolUse decision control](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#pretooluse-decision-control).

PermissionRequest.description: Fires when a tool requests user permission. Matcher filters by tool name. See [PermissionRequest decision control](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#permissionrequest-decision-control).

PostToolUse.description: Fires after a tool executes successfully. Matcher filters by tool name. Supports [async hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#run-hooks-in-the-background).

PostToolUseFailure.description: Fires after a tool execution fails. Matcher filters by tool name. Supports [async hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#run-hooks-in-the-background).

Notification.description: Fires on notifications. Matcher filters by type: `permission_prompt`, `idle_prompt`, `auth_success`, `elicitation_dialog`.

SubagentStart.description: Fires when a subagent is spawned. Matcher filters by agent type.

SubagentStop.description: Fires when a subagent stops. Matcher filters by agent type.

Stop.description: Fires when Claude's turn ends. **No matcher support** — always fires.

TeammateIdle.description: Fires when an [agent team](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams) teammate is about to go idle. **No matcher support**.

TaskCompleted.description: Fires when a task is being marked as completed. **No matcher support**.

ConfigChange.description: Fires when a configuration file changes during a session. Matcher filters by source: `user_settings`, `project_settings`, `local_settings`, `policy_settings`, `skills`.

PreCompact.description: Fires before context compaction. Matcher filters by trigger type: `manual`, `auto`.

SessionEnd.description: Fires when a session ends. Matcher filters by exit reason: `clear`, `logout`, `prompt_input_exit`, `bypass_permissions_disabled`, `other`.

MatcherGroup.description: A matcher group pairs an optional regex filter with an array of hook handlers. If `matcher` is omitted, the hooks fire on every occurrence of the event. See [matcher patterns](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#matcher-patterns).

matcher.description: Regex pattern to filter when this group's hooks fire. Use `|` for OR, `.*` for wildcards. MCP tools follow the pattern `mcp__<server>__<tool>`. Omit to match all.

CommandHook.description: Runs a shell command or script. Receives [hook context as JSON on stdin](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#common-input-fields). **Exit 0**: success (stdout parsed as JSON). **Exit 2**: blocking error (stderr fed to Claude). **Other**: non-blocking error.

command.description: Shell command or script path. Use `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` for the project root (always quote it) and `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` for plugin-relative paths.

statusMessage.description: Custom spinner message displayed while the hook runs.

async.description: Run in background without blocking. Only for `PostToolUse`/`PostToolUseFailure`. See [async hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#run-hooks-in-the-background).

once.description: Run once per session then remove. Primarily useful in skill-scoped hooks.

PromptHook.description: Evaluates a single-turn LLM [prompt-based hook](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#prompt-based-hooks). Must respond with `{ok: true}` or `{ok: false, reason: "..."}`. Cannot use tools.

prompt.description: The evaluation prompt. Use `$ARGUMENTS` to interpolate the hook's full input context as JSON.

model.description: Model for evaluation. Defaults to a fast model.

statusMessage.description: Custom spinner message displayed while the hook runs.

once.description: Run once per session then remove.

AgentHook.description: Runs a multi-turn LLM [agent-based hook](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks#agent-based-hooks) with tool access. Must respond with `{ok: true}` or `{ok: false, reason: "..."}`. Can use `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `Bash`, etc.

prompt.description: The verification prompt. Use `$ARGUMENTS` to interpolate the hook's input context as JSON.

model.description: Model for the agent. Defaults to a fast model.

statusMessage.description: Custom spinner message displayed while the hook runs.

once.description: Run once per session then remove.
